title: hieracium murorum subsp. erucifolium
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. erucifolium, also known as Hawkweed or Mouse Ear Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. erucifolium include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of Hieracium murorum subsp. erucifolium include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal properties.
